name: Natural pools of Riofrío – circular from Villasrubias
name_original: Piscinas naturales del Riofrío – circular desde Villasrubias
region_intro: Hiking around Villasrubias offers access to diverse landscapes within the province of Salamanca, Spain. The region is characterized by its natural environment, featuring historical trails that once served as trade routes. The terrain includes varied paths, from established routes to more challenging mountain areas, indicative of the broader Salamanca and Castile and Leon region. These natural settings provide a scenic backdrop for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Villasrubias The most popular hiking route is Natural pools of Riofrío – circular from Villasrubias, a 2.3 miles (3.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour to complete. This easy route offers a gentle path leading to natural pools. Another top favourite among local hikers is Torre Almenara from Gata — circular by Camino de la Almenara, a difficult 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Local hikers also love the Descargamaría to Ovejuela — circular via Robledillo de Gata, a 13.7 miles (22.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 7 hours 4 minutes. Hiking around Villasrubias is defined by its beautiful landscapes, historical trails, and diverse terrain, offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Villasrubias are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Villasrubias's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Villasrubias?
A: There are over 15 hiking routes around Villasrubias, offering a variety of experiences. These include 4 easy routes, 9 moderate routes, and 5 difficult routes, catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Villasrubias?
A: Yes, for an easy and family-friendly option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993198451/natural-pools-of-riofrio-circular-from-villasrubias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pools of Riofrío – circular from Villasrubias&lt;/a&gt;. This 3.7 km route takes about 1 hour to complete and leads to natural pools, perfect for a gentle outing.

Q: What kind of natural landmarks can I explore while hiking near Villasrubias?
A: The region around Villasrubias features several natural attractions. You can visit the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6053913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrituelo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4840158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riofrío Natural Pool&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6053456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Pool of Descargamaría&lt;/a&gt;. These spots offer scenic beauty and opportunities for relaxation.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes with panoramic views?
A: Yes, for a challenging circular route with panoramic views, try &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1268811546/torre-almenara-from-gata-circular-by-camino-de-la-almenara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre Almenara from Gata — circular by Camino de la Almenara&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 9.8 km trail features significant elevation gain, rewarding hikers with expansive vistas.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Villasrubias?
A: The hiking routes in Villasrubias are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the beautiful landscapes, diverse terrain, and the historical significance of many trails.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Experienced trekkers can explore routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1278066129/descargamaria-to-ovejuela-circular-via-robledillo-de-gata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Descargamaría to Ovejuela — circular via Robledillo de Gata&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 22 km trail leads through varied terrain and typically takes over 7 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any historical sites or settlements to see on the trails?
A: Yes, the region has historical connections. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4821468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Almenara&lt;/a&gt; is a notable landmark. Additionally, some routes pass through charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6054062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledillo de Gata&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into local history and culture.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Villasrubias?
A: The region's 'beautiful landscapes and nature' suggest that spring and autumn would be ideal for hiking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be hot, while winters might be cooler but still suitable for hiking.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, you can find scenic viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6968398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de los Ángeles Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which offers expansive views of the surrounding natural environment. Another challenging route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1272063273/mirador-de-la-maja-de-la-sierra-from-gato-circular-by-praderas-de-el-pantano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Majá de la Sierra from Gato — circular by Praderas de el Pantano&lt;/a&gt;, also promises rewarding vistas.

Q: Are there any very long-distance routes in the area?
A: For a significant long-distance challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1277981665/cadalso-to-descargamaria-circular-via-the-gr-10-e03" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cadalso to Descargamaría — circular via the GR 10 E03&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult route spans 27 km and can take over 7 hours to complete, traversing varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trails around Villasrubias?
A: The terrain around Villasrubias is diverse, ranging from established paths to more challenging mountain areas. You can expect a mix of natural environments, characteristic of the Salamanca region, providing varied experiences for hikers.
tips: This dam that gives rise to the natural pools downstream of the Riofrío is a very quiet place and ideal for the summer months since you can take a refreshing bath in its waters.
An ideal and popular place in the summer months, since here you will find everything you need to spend a good day by the water and take a refreshing bath in a very picturesque and relaxing landscape.
